title: Specified Professional — Presumptive Income u/s 44ADA (No Salary)
description: Self-employed specified professional (CA, doctor, lawyer, architect, engineer, film artist, interior decorator, authorised representative) with gross receipts ≤ ₹75 lakh, opting for presumptive taxation under Section 44ADA. Declares 50% of gross receipts as income. No need to maintain books of accounts. Uses ITR-4.

prevalence.description: Common among independent professionals, especially in Tier 2/3 cities.

typical_evidence.notes: AIS is the primary source for professional receipts. Look for professional income entries. Clients who paid > ₹30,000 per engagement may have deducted TDS u/s 194J.
26AS for TDS u/s 194J (professional/technical services, 10%). Multiple clients may have deducted TDS.
Form 16A from clients who deducted TDS. Should match 26AS.

applicable_decisions.notes: Presumptive professional income under 44ADA → ITR-4 if no disqualifiers and total income ≤ ₹50L.
Confirm 44ADA eligibility: specified profession, gross receipts ≤ ₹75L, resident status, no disqualifiers.

Regime selection. For 44ADA professionals, 50% of receipts is the income (the other 50% is deemed expenses). The actual expense claim is irrelevant in presumptive — so the Old Regime's deductions only apply to the net income figure.
TDS from clients u/s 194J. Multiple clients means multiple TDS entries in 26AS.
Advance tax is relevant here — self-employed professionals are required to pay advance tax if tax liability > ₹10,000. Many miss this.

common_validations.notes: Form 16A from clients vs 26AS reconciliation. Common when clients file quarterly TDS returns late.
Professional receipts in AIS must all be accounted for. AIS may show the gross payment, which equals the receipts for 44ADA purposes.
Advance tax may be due — check if advance tax was paid in 26AS Part C.

intake_questions.question: What is your profession?
intake_questions.purpose: Confirm the profession is in the specified list for 44ADA eligibility. If not listed, 44ADA does not apply — must use 44AD or maintain books.
answer_options: Chartered Accountant / Company Secretary / Cost Accountant

intake_questions.question: What were your total gross receipts from your profession in FY 2024-25 (before any deductions)?
Do you want to declare income lower than 50% of your gross receipts?
intake_questions.purpose: If actual income is < 50% of receipts, books must be maintained and audit may be required. Presumptive scheme is not available.

intake_questions.question: Did you pay advance tax in FY 2024-25?
intake_questions.purpose: Self-employed professionals are required to pay advance tax if liability > ₹10,000. Non-payment triggers interest u/s 234B/234C.

edge_cases.consequence: 44ADA threshold breached. Books of accounts must be maintained. Audit required if income < 50% of receipts. Must use ITR-3.
Pattern upgrades to PAT-002 (Salary + Gig). Salary + 44ADA professional income → still ITR-4.

edge_cases.condition: Previously opted out of 44ADA (declared income < 50% in a prior year)
edge_cases.consequence: Once opted out, the professional cannot use 44ADA for 5 assessment years. Must maintain books and use ITR-3.

notes: This pattern is for pure self-employment. No salary. If the professional has any salary from a part-time employment, PAT-002 applies instead. Advance tax is the most commonly missed obligation in this pattern.
